methods.method_name: Lake Sediment Core Collection and Chronology
methods.description: Process for retrieving lake sediment cores from Lake Challa, creating a continuous composite sequence, and establishing its chronology using radiocarbon dating and other methods.

steps.description: In 2003 and 2005, 22 m-long lake-sediment cores were retrieved from a central location (Fig. 1b) in Lake Challa (Verschuren et al., 2009).
steps.searchable_summary: retrieve lake sediment cores

steps.description: Cross-correlation of the overlapping core sections produced a continuous, 20.82 m-long composite sequence.
steps.searchable_summary: create composite core sequence from overlapping sections

steps.description: Its chronology was established through high-resolution 14 C dating of bulk organic matter...
steps.searchable_summary: establish core chronology using 14C dating

steps.description: ...corrected for an evolving old-carbon ageoffset ('reservoir effect') by comparing overlapping 210 Pb and 14 C dates...
steps.searchable_summary: correct 14C dates for reservoir effect using 210Pb comparison

steps.description: ...paired 14 C dates on bulk organic matter and terrestrial plant macrofossils...
steps.searchable_summary: correct 14C dates using paired bulk organic matter and plant macrofossil dates

steps.description: ...and wiggle-matching well-dated core sections with 14 C anomalies in the INTCAL04 curve (Blaauw et al., 2011).
steps.searchable_summary: refine chronology using wiggle-matching with INTCAL04 curve

methods.description: Method for preparing lake sediment samples to isolate diatom frustules and analyzing the isolated material for its d13C value and percentage of total organic carbon (%TOC).

steps.description: For this study, a total of 249 samples at mostly constant intervals of 8 cm (w100 years, on average) were selected for determination of d 13 C diatom .
steps.searchable_summary: select sediment samples from core

steps.description: In brief, samples were treated with HCl, H 2 O 2 and HNO 3 to remove carbonates and external organic matter.
steps.searchable_summary: chemically treat sediment samples to remove carbonates and organic matter

steps.description: Samples were then sieved at 63 mm, 38 mm and 20 mm to remove mineral matter and sponge spicules, resulting in >90% pure diatom frustules...
steps.searchable_summary: sieve samples to isolate diatom frustules

steps.description: ...as verified by SEM and light microscopy (Hurrell, 2009).
steps.searchable_summary: verify diatom purity using microscopy

steps.description: The percentage organic carbon left after this preparation (%TOC diatom ) and its d 13 C value were analysed using an online system comprised of a Costech ECS4010 elemental analyser, a VG TripleTrap, and a VG Optima mass spectrometer.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze %TOC and d13C of diatom samples using elemental analyzer and mass spectrometer

steps.description: Each analytical run contained replicates of the laboratory primary standard (BROC) and a secondary low percentage C standard (SOILB).
steps.searchable_summary: include laboratory standards in analytical runs

steps.description: d 13 C diatom values were calibrated to the VPDB (Vienna Pee Dee Belemnite) standard using reference samples NBS-19 and NBS-22.
steps.searchable_summary: calibrate d13C values to VPDB standard

steps.description: TOC analysis of the cleaned diatom samples showed that 22 samples contained 1e4% carbon, opening the possibility of residual organic matter external to the diatom frustule and therefore these samples were rejected...
steps.searchable_summary: reject samples based on high %TOC threshold

steps.description: Interpretation of the down-core variation in d 13 C diatom is based on the 227 samples with % TOC diatom < 1.0.
steps.searchable_summary: select samples for interpretation based on low %TOC threshold

methods.method_name: TOC, TN, and d13C Bulk Analysis
methods.description: Analysis of Total Organic Carbon (TOC), Total Nitrogen (TN), and bulk d13C in sediment samples. (Note: Specific analytical details are not provided in the text).

steps.description: The TOC, total nitrogen (TN) and d 13 C bulk were analysed...
steps.searchable_summary: analyze TOC, TN, and d13C bulk in sediment samples

methods.description: Method for measuring Biogenic Silica (BSi) concentration in sediment samples.

steps.description: BSi was measured by digesting sediment samples in 1% Na 2 CO 3 for 3 h at 85 C in a heated shaking bath.
steps.searchable_summary: digest sediment samples for BSi measurement

steps.description: Sub-samples were taken...
steps.searchable_summary: take sub-samples from digested solution

steps.description: ...and neutralised with HCl...
steps.searchable_summary: neutralize sub-samples with HCl

steps.description: ...then measured for the dissolved silica extracted (Conley and Schelske, 2001).
steps.searchable_summary: measure dissolved silica concentration

steps.description: Increases in dissolved silica concentrations did not occur through time (D. Conley, unpubl. data) and, therefore, corrections for mineral dissolution were not made (Conley, 1998).
steps.searchable_summary: do not apply corrections for mineral dissolution based on test results
inputs: Dissolved silica concentration values over time

methods.method_name: Diatom Assemblage Enumeration and Species Control
methods.description: Method for counting fossil diatom valves in sediment samples to determine species composition for palaeoecological interpretation and to control for species-specific isotopic effects.

steps.description: Diatoms were enumerated for palaeoecological interpretation using standard methods.
steps.searchable_summary: enumerate fossil diatoms using standard methods

steps.description: Percentage composition of fossil diatom assemblages based on counts of >400 valves were presented by Milne (2007)...
steps.searchable_summary: determine percentage composition based on counts of >400 valves

steps.description: ...whereas here we report only on the two major taxa that comprise over 90% of the assemblages; Gomphocymbella and Nitzschia spp.
steps.searchable_summary: report on major diatom taxa (Gomphocymbella, Nitzschia spp.)

steps.description: Additional counts of 200 valves were made following the cleaning process for isotope analysis, to control for species effects on d 13 C diatom (Hurrell, 2009).
steps.searchable_summary: make additional diatom counts (200 valves) for isotope control
outputs: Additional diatom counts (200 valves per sample)

methods.description: Statistical analysis performed to calculate moving-average correlations between the d13C diatom and d13C bulk time series.

steps.description: For this purpose the higher-resolution d 13 C bulk data were re-sampled at intervals corresponding to the 227 d 13 C diatom samples.
steps.searchable_summary: resample higher-resolution data to match lower-resolution data intervals

steps.description: Moving-average correlations between d 13 C diatom and d 13 C bulk were performed using the Pearson product moment correlation function in Microsoft Excel.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate moving-average Pearson correlation

steps.description: Each window was constrained to be equivalent to 3 ka...
steps.searchable_summary: set moving window size based on age
outputs: Moving window size (in number of samples)

steps.description: ...and contained a minimum of 24 samples.
steps.searchable_summary: ensure minimum number of samples per window
inputs: Moving window size (in number of samples)

steps.description: ...to ensure a large enough sample size to enable statistical significance estimates to be made.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate statistical significance of correlations
